intro: Converting from C++ to Kotlin moves an application from a hardware-centric, manually optimized landscape to the managed, garbage-collected JVM or Kotlin/Native ecosystems. Kotlin replaces C++ manual resource tracking and pointers with absolute type safety, zero-overhead nullability tracking, and clean object orientation. This migration improves developer velocity and memory safety while utilizing Kotlin's lightweight concurrency primitives.

diffs.from: Static, nominal, and structural system with unchecked conversions, template metaprogramming, and raw pointer reinterpretation.
diffs.to: Static, nominal system featuring explicit nullability annotations, smart casts, generics with variance, and sealed class hierarchies.

diffs.from: Verbosely expressive with semicolons, explicit memory operators (*, &), forward declarations, and header inclusion syntax.
diffs.to: Clean, highly expressive layout eliminating semicolons, header files, and boilerplate properties via data classes and concise lambdas.

diffs.from: Determined via stack allocations, strict block-scoped RAII lifecycles, and manual or reference-counted heap allocations.
diffs.to: Managed primarily by the JVM Garbage Collector (or ARC/tracing GC in Kotlin/Native), abstracting explicit allocations entirely.

diffs.from: Managed via std::thread, platform threads, POSIX primitives, or heavy third-party event loops like libuv.
diffs.to: Powered by Coroutines, providing non-blocking, sequential-looking asynchronous code through suspend functions and structured channels/flows.

diffs.from: Provides low-level containers, memory utilities, basic algorithmic functions, and direct OS wrapper utilities.
diffs.to: Rich collection API, functional utilities, cross-platform IO abstractions, and extensive string manipulation frameworks.

examples.title: Data Containment Class
examples.description: Compares a boilerplate-heavy C++ data class containing custom equality and hashing with a Kotlin data class.
examples.toCode: data class Product(val name: String, val price: Double)

examples.title: Null Safety and Optional Value Handling
examples.description: Contrasts checking for null pointers or std::optional in C++ with Kotlin's safe call operator and Elvis operator.

tips.title: Replace Templates with Generics
tips.body: C++ templates generate code at compile time per type instance. Kotlin generics use type erasure (on JVM) and enforce variance (in/out). Redesign template metaprogramming into interface-driven design.

tips.title: Adopt Data Classes for State
tips.body: Stop writing custom copy constructors, assignment operators, and hash functions for simple objects. Use Kotlin's 'data class' keyword to auto-generate these utilities safely.

tips.title: Handle Destructors with Closeable
tips.body: Kotlin lacks RAII scoping for stack objects. Replace deterministic C++ destructors by implementing the 'AutoCloseable' interface and wrapping usage inside the '.use {}' extension function.

tips.title: Bridge native assets via JNI/JNA
tips.body: If porting incrementally, use Java Native Interface (JNI) or Kotlin/Native interoperability layers to call legacy C++ shared libraries (.so/.dll) without breaking existing performance-critical modules.
